name: Gordon Jenkins
description: American arranger, composer, and pianist who was influential in popular music in the 1940s and 1950s
abstract: Gordon Hill Jenkins (May 12, 1910 – May 1, 1984) was an American arranger, composer, and pianist who was influential in popular music in the 1940s and 1950s. Jenkins worked with The Andrews Sisters, Johnny Cash, The Weavers, Frank Sinatra, Louis Armstrong, Judy Garland, Nat King Cole, Billie Holiday, Harry Nilsson, Peggy Lee and Ella Fit…

works_created.t: Manhattan Tower: New York's My Home
When a Woman Loves a Man

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: You know who was the arranger? He became big later. Gordon Jenkins. Gordon Jenkins was the arranger. So I stayed with him until let’s say around July 7th or 8th... We toured all of New England with Jones.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gallodoro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Oneonta, New York, December 28, 2005

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: I got a chance to do some things for Decca Records, Gordon Jenkins, toward the end of '49... Whatever they needed; vocals mostly. Gordon Jenkins was sort of a house man there.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: I also learned a lot about accompanying singers by listening to the arrangements that Billy May and Heimey Bow and Gordon Jenkins and Nelson Riddle did for singers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I had just been through two months of re-orchestrating all Nelson Riddle’s arrangements and Gordon Jenkins and all these things for [Count] Basie’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Davis
about_quotes.evidence: whether you’re playing a Billy May chart or a Nelson Riddle chart... or Gordon Jenkins or Don Costa. These — they were all — every chart is a 10.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Georg Brunis
about_quotes.evidence: And this arranger used to come in there. He was a big man, Gordon Jenkins. And he'd sit alongside of me and I'd play them low notes.
